name: Sourwood
summary: Acid-soil specialist (pH 4.0–6.0) — pairs perfectly with Pocono Plateau soils. Drooping clusters of small white bell flowers in July–August yield the famed 'sourwood honey' and fill the late-summer pollinator dearth. Brilliant scarlet fall color.
notes: Northern range edge currently at southern PA; Zone 5–9 hardy with Zone 6a at the cusp. A textbook climate-winner expected to expand northward. Slow-growing, single-trunked small tree (20–40 ft). Source seed or stock from northern-edge provenances (NJ Highlands, southeastern PA) for cold-hardiness.

planting.aspect: Sheltered openings; gap edges; partial-shade sites
planting.moisture: Well-drained mesic; not for wet sites
planting.site_notes: Climate-trial planting — start with 3–5 trees in a sheltered location and observe survival before scaling. Source northern-provenance stock if possible. Wire cage; moderate deer browse on young saplings.
